meta.usage: Recognition-stratified DIAGNOSTIC set (codebook s8 v1.1; zero-human amendment). None of these items appears in the coder prompt. Fetch real texts at pilot, sanitize, code blind. Report accuracy ONLY stratified by the validation-battery sB5 identifiability score; zero headline construct-validity weight (these are famous, memorization-suspect papers coded by the hypothesis-aware instrument designer). Construct validity lives in the synthetic criterion set (validation-battery sB3). Gold codes unchanged from v1.0. v1.2 (2026-07-16): dk re-adjudication of A04/A05/A07 from sanitized text alone, triggered by the unison-miss gold audit — see codebook s8 audit rule and s12 changelog v1.4b. v1.3 (2026-07-16, unison audit round 2): A06 S2->S1 (abstract-vs-paper gap; instrument codes the abstract), A12 S1->S2 (edge anchor: design-argument target), A04 D3 reviewed and SUSTAINED (DEF).
meta.coverage_gaps: S6: no anchor (promote first clear pilot item to A21). CS5: no in-corpus anchor (known exemplars live outside corpus venues; recorded as expected-distribution observation).

anchors.citation: Rowe, 'The Problem of Evil and Some Varieties of Atheism', American Philosophical Quarterly 16 (1979), 335-341
anchors.rationale: Positive evidential claim from apparently gratuitous suffering; observational premise is load-bearing (deletion test fails without it).

anchors.citation: Draper, 'Pain and Pleasure: An Evidential Problem for Theists', Nous 23 (1989), 331-350
anchors.rationale: Comparative likelihood of observed pain/pleasure distribution on indifference vs theism; qualitative comparison, so CS4 not CS5.

anchors.citation: Wykstra, 'The Humean Obstacle to Evidential Arguments from Suffering', International Journal for Philosophy of Religion 16 (1984), 73-93
anchors.rationale: CORNEA undercuts the noseeum inference; rebuts the evidential argument without positive evidence for goodness.

anchors.citation: Law, 'The Evil-God Challenge', Religious Studies 46 (2010), 353-373
anchors.verification: web-verified 2026-07-14 (cited in RS 52 reference list) · dk re-adjudication 2026-07-16 (sanitized text; D4 alpha->beta under the multi-fatal-premise dominance rule) · dk review 2026-07-16 (unison audit round 2: coders' unanimous POS reviewed; gold DEF SUSTAINED — the parity claim is an undercutter of the goodness claim's reasonableness, not new disconfirming evidence)
anchors.rationale: Symmetry undercutter: defenses of good-god equally save evil-god. D4 beta (v1.2): the observed mixture of good and evil is a deletion-fatal empirical premise alongside the conceptual symmetry principle; dominance rule codes beta.

anchors.citation: Schellenberg, 'The hiddenness argument revisited (I)', Religious Studies 41.2 (2005), 201-215, DOI 10.1017/S0034412505007614 [companion: '(II)', 41.3 (2005), 287-303, DOI 10.1017/S0034412505007791]
anchors.verification: web-verified 2026-07-14 (Crossref; part I/II structure confirmed) · dk re-adjudication 2026-07-16 (sanitized text; was coded from the 1993 argument's reputation, not this paper — designer memorization error caught by unison-miss audit)
anchors.rationale: Reply-chain defense of the hiddenness argument: rebuts critics' objections as irrelevant (defeater-rebuttal, CS2/DEF), carried by conceptual analysis of the objections (alpha). Net effect contra the S5 thesis; edge-anchor rule routes hiddenness to S5.

anchors.citation: Morriston, 'Must the Beginning of the Universe Have a Personal Cause?', Faith and Philosophy 17 (2000), 149-169
anchors.verification: audited (conversation record) · dk re-adjudication 2026-07-16 (unison audit round 2: abstract foregrounds the causal-principle challenge; the paper's S2 thesis is under-represented in the abstract — instrument codes the abstract, so S1)
anchors.rationale: Challenges the kalam causal principle ('whatever begins to exist must have a cause') as stated in the abstract's primary conclusion — contra S1, defeater-rebuttal (CS2/DEF), conceptual (alpha). Paper-level S2 material noted as abstract-vs-paper gap.

anchors.citation: Swinburne, 'The Argument from Design', Philosophy 43.165 (1968), 199-212, DOI 10.1017/S0031819100009189
anchors.verification: web-verified 2026-07-14 (Cambridge Core + Crossref) · dk re-adjudication 2026-07-16 (sanitized text; same memorization-error class as A05)
anchors.rationale: Abstract announces a defeater-rebuttal: no formal objection of Hume's Philo is valid against a carefully articulated design argument (CS2/DEF, conceptual — alpha). Former anti-folklore role (pro/CS4/beta exemplar) is superseded by synthetic criterion cells iii/iv.

anchors.citation: Craig, 'Professor Mackie and the Kalam Cosmological Argument', Religious Studies 20 (1984), 367-375
anchors.rationale: Defends the kalam argument by rebutting Mackie's objections; net effect on S1 thesis positive; no new positive evidence.

anchors.citation: Oppy, 'Craig, Mackie, and the Kalam Cosmological Argument', Religious Studies 27 (1991), 189-197
anchors.rationale: Reply-to-a-reply: defends Mackie's objections against Craig's rebuttal; tracing the chain, net effect on S1 thesis is negative. Canonical net-effect-rule teacher.

anchors.citation: Malpass & Morriston, 'Endless and Infinite', Philosophical Quarterly 70 (2020)
anchors.rationale: Rebuts the successive-addition case against an infinite past; undercuts a kalam premise without asserting the universe is uncaused.

anchors.citation: White, 'Fine-Tuning and Multiple Universes', Nous 34 (2000), 260-276
anchors.rationale: FUNCTIONAL DIRECTION: rebuts the multiverse rejoinder to fine-tuning (inverse gambler's fallacy); net effect pro the S1 thesis regardless of author's worldview. Correction of outline v0.2 shelving.

anchors.citation: McGrew, McGrew & Vestrup, 'Probabilities and the Fine-Tuning Argument: A Sceptical View', Mind 110 (2001)
anchors.verification: audited (conversation record) · dk re-adjudication 2026-07-16 (unison audit round 2: abstract frames the target as cosmological DESIGN arguments — designer-agent conclusion, edge anchor routes to S2)
anchors.rationale: Normalizability problem undercuts the design/fine-tuning argument's probability machinery; abstract targets designer-agent conclusions (edge anchor: design -> S2). A priori measure-theoretic point is load-bearing.

anchors.citation: Rasmussen, 'From a Necessary Being to God', International Journal for Philosophy of Religion 66 (2009)
anchors.rationale: Positive a priori case that a necessary being would have divine attributes; qualitative support for the S3 bridge.

anchors.citation: Gwiazda, 'Richard Swinburne's argument to the simplicity of God via the infinite', Religious Studies 45 (2009), 487-493
anchors.verification: web-verified 2026-07-14 (Cambridge Core abstract; Swinburne reply 495-498 same issue)
anchors.rationale: Rebuts the simplicity inference to a maximal unique being by exposing a tension in Swinburne's construal of the omni-properties.

anchors.citation: Plantinga, 'Is Belief in God Properly Basic?', Nous 15.1 (1981), 41-51, DOI 10.2307/2215239
anchors.rationale: Bundled God-target; claims warrant without propositional evidence - the canonical CS3 pattern.

anchors.citation: Bostrom, 'Are You Living in a Computer Simulation?', Philosophical Quarterly 53 (2003), 243-255
anchors.rationale: No chain-step thesis supported or opposed; witness-role papers are framework citations, not corpus evidence. Teaches the X code.

anchors.citation: Tuggy, 'The Unfinished Business of Trinitarian Theorizing', Religious Studies 39 (2003)
anchors.rationale: Argues extant Trinity theories fail their coherence task; rebuts the coherence defenses rather than adducing evidence against the doctrine's truth.

anchors.citation: Brower & Rea, 'Material Constitution and the Trinity', Faith and Philosophy 22 (2005)
anchors.rationale: Coherence-only: a constitution model on which the Trinity is consistent; no claim of evidence for its truth. Canonical CS1.

anchors.citation: Swinburne, 'Natural Theology, Its Dwindling Probabilities and Lack of Rapport', Faith and Philosophy 21 (2004), 533-546
anchors.rationale: Method-level: rebuts Plantinga's structural objection to ramified natural theology (evidence entering at stages); defends the case-making machinery, not a step.

anchors.citation: Allison, 'The Resurrection of Jesus: Apologetics, Polemics, History', T&T Clark (2021) [book]
anchors.rationale: Explicit non liquet after weighing testimony and rivals: the canonical neutral/verdict-suspension pattern, historical-documentary type.
